name: Architext 1.7.7 Doctor Non-Interactive Guard
summary: Safety patch for `architext doctor`. The doctor repair confirmation defaulted to Yes and treated EOF on stdin as the default answer, so any non-interactive shell (agent session, CI, cron, piped stdin) silently consented to deterministic repairs that mutate tracked data files — observed writing docs/architext/data/releases/index.json from a headless session. Doctor now refuses to apply repairs when stdin is not a terminal unless --yes is passed explicitly: it reports that repairs are pending and points at --yes / --dry-run without writing anything. Inside the interactive prompt, EOF or a read error now declines instead of taking the default. Intentional behavior change: `echo y | architext doctor` no longer consents; scripts must use --yes.
updateSource: Shipped 2026-07-16: GitHub release v1.7.7 published 2026-07-16T08:52:04Z with all five native binaries + SHA256SUMS attached by publish.yml. Install path verified end to end: the darwin-arm64 asset downloaded, its SHA256 matched the manifest, and the binary reports 1.7.7. Fix verified pre-release on a fixture with a pending release-truth repair (EOF stdin, piped y, and --dry-run leave the tree untouched; --yes applies); CLI suite 37/37 with three new consent tests; verify-rust green on PRs #254/#255/#256.

required.title: doctor never auto-accepts its repair prompt in a non-interactive shell
required.summary: Root cause: prompt_yes_no defaulted to Yes and read_line at EOF returned an empty answer that fell through to the default, making 'no terminal attached' indistinguishable from 'user pressed Enter to accept'. Fix in crates/architext-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs: (1) when stdin is not a TTY and --yes was not passed, doctor refuses to prompt at all — it reports repairs are pending and exits without writing, pointing at --yes / --dry-run; (2) EOF or a read error at the prompt declines instead of taking the default (a live TTY can still send Ctrl-D); (3) consent parsing extracted to read_yes_no_answer and unit-tested. Behavior change: piped stdin (echo y |) no longer consents — non-interactive callers must pass --yes.

deferred.title: Apply side skips the advertised 'create missing Release Truth history index' repair
deferred.summary: Discovered while verifying this fix: doctor's status side advertises a 'create missing Release Truth history index' repair when manifest.files.releases points at an absent index file, but repair_release_truth_data returns an empty change list in that case — so `doctor --yes` prints 'Applied doctor repairs:' with no entries and the file is never created. Deferred because it is a separate apply-side defect requiring its own index-regeneration logic and tests, independent of the consent guard shipped here; it does not weaken the guard and carries no data-loss risk (nothing is written). Queued as the first work item for a /swarm session.

workstreams.name: CLI safety
workstreams.summary: Make doctor's mutating repair path require explicit consent: TTY-or---yes gate plus EOF-declines prompt semantics.

evidence.label: read_yes_no_answer unit tests: EOF never consents, explicit y/n respected, bare Enter takes the default (cargo test -p architext-cli, 37/37)
Four-mode fixture verification on a data copy with a pending release-truth repair: EOF stdin, piped y, and --dry-run leave the tree untouched; --yes applies
PR #254 verify-rust CI green (7m8s) and merged to develop
